articles.title: Montana sports betting revenue remains steady, despite rise of prediction market apps
articles.summary: Montana's sports betting market is holding firm even as prediction market apps like Kalshi and Polymarket gain traction nationwide. The steady revenue suggests traditional sports betting and prediction markets are attracting different user bases rather than cannibalizing each other.

articles.title: Should You Be Using Polymarket to Invest in Crypto?
articles.summary: Traders are debating whether Polymarket offers a viable path for crypto investing beyond traditional exchanges and DeFi protocols. The answer reveals how prediction markets are evolving from pure speculation into alternative investment vehicles that could reshape where smart money flows.

articles.title: Is Polymarket a threat to democracy? The money is simply too good
articles.summary: Critics are raising alarms that Polymarket's massive trading volumes and profit incentives could distort democratic processes by turning elections into gambling spectacles. The debate matters because regulatory crackdowns on "democracy gambling" could reshape how prediction markets operate during future election cycles.

articles.title: ‘Cowboy’: Michael Selig, a top Trump Wall Street regulator, comes out swinging
articles.summary: Michael Selig, a key Trump-era Wall Street regulator, is making aggressive moves that signal a major shift in financial oversight policy. His confrontational approach could reshape market regulations and create new volatility in financial sector stocks, making regulatory policy bets suddenly very interesting.

articles.title: Prediction markets surge, but Wall Street giants remain on sidelines (KALSHI:Private)
articles.summary: Prediction markets are hitting new highs while major Wall Street firms sit out the action, despite the obvious potential for institutional money to flood these platforms. The holdout creates a massive opportunity for retail traders and smaller firms to grab market share before the big players inevitably pile in and squeeze margins.

articles.title: New Kalshi Referral Code SILIVE: $10 The Masters bonus; Steph Curry odds update
articles.summary: Kalshi launched a new referral code "SILIVE" offering a $10 bonus tied to The Masters tournament, while updating odds on basketball star Steph Curry. The promotion signals Kalshi's push to capture sports betting crossover users during major tournaments, using celebrity athlete props to drive user acquisition in the prediction markets space.

articles.title: ‘Abhorrent’: the inside story of the Polymarket gamblers betting millions on war
articles.summary: Polymarket users are placing massive bets on war outcomes, sparking outrage over the ethics of profiting from human tragedy. The controversy threatens to fuel regulatory crackdowns on prediction markets, especially as platforms struggle to balance free speech with public relations disasters.

articles.title: Robinhood excludes some prediction markets over manipulation fears
articles.summary: Robinhood pulled certain prediction markets from its platform citing concerns about potential manipulation. The move signals that mainstream brokers are still skittish about market integrity issues that could bring regulatory heat, potentially slowing broader adoption of prediction betting.

articles.title: US judge blocks Arizona criminal case against Kalshi at CFTC's request
articles.summary: A federal judge halted Arizona's criminal prosecution of prediction market Kalshi after the CFTC asked the court to intervene. This signals the feds want to handle Kalshi's regulatory issues themselves rather than let states pile on with criminal charges that could fragment oversight of prediction markets.

articles.title: Prediction markets ramp up DC messaging
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms are launching aggressive lobbying and PR campaigns to influence policymakers in Washington. The push signals these companies know regulatory clarity is coming soon and want to shape the rules before bureaucrats write them without industry input.

articles.title: Prediction markets have made betting easier than ever—and young men are paying the price
articles.summary: Young men are getting hooked on prediction markets and losing money as platforms make wagering more accessible and gamified. The trend threatens regulatory backlash that could kill the prediction market boom before it reaches mainstream adoption.

articles.title: Pile of Famous Athletes Quietly Invested in Kalshi Months Ago
articles.summary: A bunch of big-name athletes dropped money into Kalshi well before the prediction market went mainstream this election cycle. These early bets from celebrities with serious cash and influence signals that prediction markets are moving from niche finance tool to legitimate investment category that attracts real money.
